title: Market Brief — Wednesday, June 10, 2026
summary: GPU oversupply is here. Util-avail stands at 20% for A100, 30% for H100, 61% for H200, and 63% for B200, meaning next-gen silicon is sitting idle at historic levels as H200 and B200 utilization cratered 58pp and 42pp respectively over 30 days. B200 spot jumped 22.9% day-over-day to $5.63/hr, a volatility spike against a backdrop of collapsing demand that screams distressed repositioning, not organic tightening.
paragraphs: GPU oversupply is here. Util-avail stands at 20% for A100, 30% for H100, 61% for H200, and 63% for B200, meaning next-gen silicon is sitting idle at historic levels as H200 and B200 utilization cratered 58pp and 42pp respectively over 30 days. B200 spot jumped 22.9% day-over-day to $5.63/hr, a volatility spike against a backdrop of collapsing demand that screams distressed repositioning, not organic tightening.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), running Llama 70B on H100 costs roughly $9.41/M tokens, yet DeepSeek V3.2 sells output at $0.34/M and Meta Llama 3.3 70B at $0.32/M, implying losses north of $9/M for any provider self-hosting on H100s at those prices. DeepSeek: Burning Cash. OpenAI gpt-oss-120b at $0.18/M output: Burning Cash. Anthropic Opus 4.6 at $25/M output: Strong. Google Gemini 2.5 Pro at $10/M: Neutral.

B200 at 63% util-avail and 211.8% annualized vol on H200 make next-gen spot grossly overvalued for anyone not running premium-tier inference. A100 at 80.5% utilization with the lowest vol at 58.5% and positive margins at high tier is the only risk-adjusted long in this market.
bullets: 61% of H200 compute capacity sits available, the loosest GPU class tracked by Ornn.
63% util-avail on B200 confirms next-gen oversupply despite a 22.9% single-day price spike.
21.78 on the LLMflation Index means frontier token prices have fallen 78% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.18/M output from OpenAI gpt-oss-120b is roughly 98% below TCO-adjusted H100 inference cost.
80.5% A100 utilization makes it the tightest compute class, down only 8.1pp over 30 days.
$5.13/M TCO-adjusted cost on B200 yields 91% margin at premium tier, the best hardware economics today.
